chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram

text: 2600 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يُحْرِمَ اَلرَّجُلُ فِي ثَوْبٍ مَصْبُوغٍ مُمَشَّقٍ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2600 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"There is no harm if a man enters Ihram wearing a dyed or striped garment."
